items.title: ‘I feel both thrilled and ruined by this’: Olivia Wilde and Edward Norton on making sex comedy The Invite
items.summary: Their movie about marital bed death is this summer’s buzziest, funniest film. Its director and her co-star talk self-loathing, psychosexuality and unexpected eruptionsEarlier this week, Edward Norton took a night flight from New York to London and felt so dreadful the next day he decided to get a massage. “I hadn’t had one in such a long time,” he says, “and I almost started crying.

items.title: Experience: I’ve found a four-leaf clover every day for three years
items.summary: Decades after my father’s death, I was still angry about losing him. Finding a good-luck symbol set me on a new path Sumter, South Carolina, where I grew up, was nicknamed “Murk City”. It’s not all bad, but it has a history of gun violence and crime.

items.title: Lisa Nandy quits X over fears Musk-owned site pushes ‘abuse and misinformation’

items.title: New interest in two things could be early sign of dementia, warns expert
items.summary: The Alzheimer's Society has also warned that a sudden change in eating behaviour, such as craving sweet, fatty foods or carbohydrates, may be an early warning sign of frontotemporal dementia

items.title: Dublin City Council opens bidding for 647 homes at five new housing developments
items.summary: Dublin City Council has launched procurement competition for 647 homes as part of their Home Building Programme, which ultimately aims to deliver 4,000 social and affordable homes

items.title: Olivia Rodrigo Extends Chart Reign In Australia
items.summary: Ella Langley continues the dominant run by female artists on the ARIA Singles Chart, a takeover that is coming up to a full year.

items.title: Madonna’s ‘Bring Your Love’ is “about saying ‘fuck you’ to doubters”, reveals Stuart Price
items.summary: The Queen Of Pop said the song emerged after she'd been told to "focus on what’s popular, algorithms, following trends", adding: "I found it to be the antithesis to art and creativity" The post Madonna’s ‘Bring Your Love’ is “about saying ‘fuck you’ to doubters”, reveals Stuart Price appeared first on NME.
